mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and the requirement for specific configurations to ensure optimal performance.

steps.title: Add the Kagenti Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Kagenti Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

```

> This command sets up the repository for installing Kagenti.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Kagenti to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/crds/kagenti.io_kagent_crd.yaml

```

> CRDs define the custom resources used by Kagenti.

steps.title: Install Kagenti using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Kagenti in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --create-namespace --version 0.5.1

```

> This installs Kagenti in its own namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource to demonstrate how to deploy an AI agent using Kagenti.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: registry.kagenti.io/example-agent:latest
EOF

```

> This creates a sample agent resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: registry.kagenti.io/example-agent:latest
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the deployed Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kagent example-agent -o yaml

```

> Look for 'status: Ready' in the output.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kagenti and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all related data and configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command removes the Kagenti release.
> This removes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get kagent -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --version 0.5.1

kubectl get kagent example-agent -o yaml

```

> Backup existing KAgent configurations.
> This command upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify the upgrade by checking the status of the example agent.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KAgent not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified KAgent resource does not exist in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the KAgent resource was created successfully and check the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image for the KAgent is not available in the container registry.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in the KAgent specification.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be insufficient permissions or resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm release status and ensure the necessary permissions are granted.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions for Kagenti were not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD installation command to ensure they are present.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new agent types and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KAgent resource with status 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get kagent'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
